files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.BaseCharacterAuthoring<TProfile>.BaseCharacterBaker<TAuthoring, TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary:
Baker for the CharacterAuthoring. Converts the authoring data into one or more ECS entities with the following setup:

  1. Creates the primary character entity (manual‐override transform) and, if Playable is checked, adds a PlayableCharacter tag.
  2. If a ModelPrefab is assigned, spawns a separate “model” entity with: – PresentationGo (holds the prefab for rendering) – ControllingEntity (points back to the character entity)
  3. Applies the authoring transform to the character via LocalTransform.
  4. If a CharacterProfile is provided: – Generates or reuses a BlobAssetReference from the profile asset (and calls DependsOn so changes re-bake automatically) – Adds LocomotionInput to drive character movement from player input or AI Agent – Builds a CapsuleCollider from profile settings (CapsuleGeometry + CollisionFilter) and adds it as a PhysicsCollider blob asset – Registers the character with the physics world (PhysicsWorldIndex) – Adds LocomotionProfileReference (points to the blob asset), LocomotionData (initial velocity, jump count, etc.), LocomotionGravity (gravity multiplier) – Initializes the CharacterLocomotionStateMachine in the Falling state

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.BaseCharacterAuthoring<TProfile>
files.Summary:
Serves as a base class for authoring character entities with locomotion capabilities. Designed to work with a profile of type TProfile, enabling customization of character movement and behavior through specific profile definitions. Contains shared properties and functionality for setting up entities related to character locomotion, including rendering models and defining state machines through further specialization. The class includes a nested abstract baker class used for converting authoring data into ECS entities with the following configurations:

  1. Associates the authored character with a profile asset (defining locomotion behavior and rules).
  2. Supports optional assignment of a prefab model entity for visual representation.
  3. Facilitates the integration of locomotion state machines, states, and custom contexts specific to the movement profile.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player.BasePlayerCharacterAuthoring<TInput>.BasePlayerCharacterBaker<TAuthoring>
files.Summary: Baker class to convert PlayerAuthoring into ECS entity.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player.BasePlayerCharacterAuthoring<TInput>
files.Summary: Abstract base class for authoring player character components. Provides support for player input assets and control over multiple characters.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player.BasePlayerCharacterBackingSystem
files.Summary: A Unity ECS system that operates under the baking system filter. This system is part of the Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player namespace and may contain logic related to the backing or processing of player character data during runtime.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player.ControlledCharactersBakingList
files.Summary: Temporary buffer element used during the baking process to store a list of entities representing controlled characters. This structure is utilized to transfer character entity references during authoring.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Authoring.Player.PlayerGoAuthoring
files.Summary: Most of the player components (camera, input, ...) still required MonoBehaviour. This authoring component has to be set on a game object prefab that will represent the game object side of the player and handle all MonoBehaviour interactions.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.Character
files.Summary: Represents a character within the locomotion system. The Character struct includes references to the locomotion and model sub-entities associated with the character. Typically used to manage and query the state of characters within gameplay systems, ensuring updates to locomotion, transformations, and other related components are synchronized.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.CharacterPath
files.Summary: A list of transform teh character went through. The first element is the next target position and the second and third element are previous transforms. Time allows for interpolation between the previous position and next target position. The third element can be used to compute velocity and acceleration.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.CharacterPhysicsProfile
files.Summary: Represents a physics configuration profile for a character, encapsulating core properties that define collision behavior, dimensions, and interaction with physics layers.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.Contact
files.Summary: Information about a collision contact point.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.ControlledCharacters
files.Summary: List of character entity controlled by the player or AI Agent

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionContact
files.Summary: Last ground or obstacle contact information.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionGravity
files.Summary: Encapsulates gravity settings for the character. This value can be updated by a system to simulate any arbitrary gravitiy direction.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionVelocity
files.Summary: Locomotion velocity for a character locomotor.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.PlayerGameObject
files.Summary: Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.PlayerGameObjectPrefab
files.Summary: Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop.

files.Title: Locomotion.Runtime.Components.TrackedMotion
files.Summary: Tracks a transform over time to compute inherited movement and velocity.

files.Title: Locomotion.Systems.PlayerCameraRigManagerSystem
files.Summary: Manages the lifecycle of the camera rig game object. It ensures it's instantiation from the prefab for every player. When a player entity gets destroyed, the cleanup component ensures the destruction of the camera rig game object.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicCharacterProfileAsset
files.Summary: A scriptable object to define the configuration for a basic character profile.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicLocomotionContext
files.Summary: Represents the context for basic locomotion mechanics, providing access to profile data, input data, state data, and base locomotion operations. This is primarily used in the state machine system for character movement and physics interactions.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicLocomotionInput
files.Summary: Input commands for the characters' locomotion.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicLocomotionProfile
files.Summary: Locomotion profiles used to define movement behavior for a character for various states of locomotion, such as moving, falling, and jumping, as well as the overall physics profile of the character. It implements the ILocomotionProfile interface to enforce the minimal configuration required for the state machine to work.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicLocomotionStateData
files.Summary: Component containing state data for basic locomotion mechanics. This struct is intended to be used within the locomotion state machine to track and manage state specific data, such as jumping count, timers, etc...

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicLocomotionStates
files.Summary: Represents the different possible locomotion states for basic movement in a character controller system. This enumeration is used within the locomotion state machine to determine the active state of an entity and manage transitions between them.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.BasicPlayerCharacterInputAsset
files.Summary: A specialized ScriptableObject representing a player's input asset for basic locomotion. This class provides the mechanisms to map inputs from a player (such as key presses or joystick motions) into meaningful locomotion values for gameplay behavior. It uses the camera's perspective to determine movement vector directions and handles actions like jumping.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.CharacterAnimationDataGatheringSystem
files.Summary: CharacterAnimationDataGatheringSystem is responsible for gathering animation data based on character locomotion states and updating the animation blackboard. This system processes the required entities and updates their animation-related components to align with their movement and state transitions.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.CharacterAnimatorSystem
files.Summary: CharacterAnimatorSystem is responsible for updating the parameters of Unity's Animator component based on data provided by the AnimationBlackBoard. This system operates within the PresentationSystemGroup, ensuring that animation updates are processed during the presentation phase of the Entity Component System (ECS) pipeline.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.FallingProfile
files.Summary: Contains parameters related to the falling behavior of a character in the locomotion system.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.JumpingProfile
files.Summary: Defines the properties associated with jumping behavior in a locomotion system.

files.Title: WAYN.Locomotion.Demo.BasicLocomotion.MovingProfile
files.Summary: Settings governing basic movement behavior (speed, acceleration, stepping, turning).

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.CharacterPath
files.Summary: A list of transform teh character went through. The first element is the next target position and the second and third element are previous transforms. Time allows for interpolation between the previous position and next target position. The third element can be used to compute velocity and acceleration.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.CharacterPhysicsProfile
files.Summary: Represents a physics configuration profile for a character, encapsulating core properties that define collision behavior, dimensions, and interaction with physics layers.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.Contact
files.Summary: Information about a collision contact point.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.ControlledCharacters
files.Summary: List of character entity controlled by the player or AI Agent

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionContact
files.Summary: Last ground or obstacle contact information.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionGravity
files.Summary: Encapsulates gravity settings for the character. This value can be updated by a system to simulate any arbitrary gravitiy direction.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.LocomotionVelocity
files.Summary: Locomotion velocity for a character locomotor.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.PlayerGameObjectInstance
files.Summary: Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.PlayerGameObjectPrefab
files.Summary: Set of references to mono objects. These are used to update and managed game object behaviors of the player entity from systems allowing for greater control on the player loop.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Components.TrackedMotion
files.Summary: Tracks a transform over time to compute inherited movement and velocity.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.BaseLocomotionContext
files.Summary: Base context available to all locomotion state machine. Contains the minimal core state data (Velocity, Position, ...) and information (Entity, Collider, PhysicsWorld,...) required for locomotion state logic and platform handling logic.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ClosestColliderCastHitCollectorIgnoreSelf
files.Summary: Struct for collecting the closest collider cast hits while ignoring a specific entity. Implements the ICollector interface to gather and manage collider cast hit results. Filters out results that do not meet defined criteria such as hits associated with an ignored entity or backface collisions. This is particularly useful in physics-related computations where certain entities need to be excluded, such as in character or object traversal and detection systems.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ClosestDistanceHitCollectorIgnoreSelf
files.Summary: Struct for collecting the closest distance-based hits while ignoring a specified entity. Implements the ICollector interface to manage distance hit results and filters hits based on custom criteria such as excluding hits involving a specified entity or backface collisions. Useful in scenarios where distance queries need to selectively exclude certain entities or object surfaces, e.g., during character or object navigation systems in physics simulations.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ClosestRaycastHitCollectorIgnoreSelf
files.Summary: Struct for collecting closest raycast hits while ignoring a specified entity. Implements the ICollector interface to manage raycast hit results and avoids processing hits that do not meet the criteria, such as hits associated with a self entity or backface collisions. Useful in scenarios where certain entities need to be ignored during raycasting computations, such as character locomotion systems in physics-based simulations.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ILocomotionContext<TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface enforcing the minimal information need for the context of locomotion state machine. It enforces the encapsulation configuration, input, and state data.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ILocomotionState<TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface for defining locomotion states within a character movement state machine.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.ILocomotionStateMachine<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface that defines the structure of a character locomotion state machine, responsible for managing state transitions, physics integration, collision resolution, and other locomotion behaviors of characters.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.LocomotionInputAsset<TInput>
files.Summary: Stores a reference to the original PlayerInputAsset used by a player.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.LocomotionInputInstance<TInput>
files.Summary: Stores a reference to the instance of a player.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.LocomotionProfileBlob<TProfile>
files.Summary: Locomotion profile in the form of a blob asset. This profile contains configuration and parameters necessary for defining locomotion behavior within a state machine.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.LocomotionStateAttribute
files.Summary: This attribute mark an enum field as a Locomotion state. This is picked up by a source generator to generate the corresponding state machine dispatch.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.StateMachine.LocomotionStateMachineAttribute
files.Summary: This attribute mark an enum as a Locomotion state machine. This is picked up by a source generator to generate the corresponding state machine component and systems.

files.Title: WAYNGames.Locomotion.Runtime.Systems.PlayerGoManagerSystem
files.Summary: Manages the lifecycle of the camera rig game object. It ensures it's instantiation from the prefab for every player. When a player entity gets destroyed, the cleanup component ensures the destruction of the camera rig game object.

files.Title: Wayn.Games.Locomotion.SourceGen.StateMachineSourceGenerator
files.Summary: A sample source generator that creates a custom report based on class properties. The target class should be annotated with the 'Generators.ReportAttribute' attribute. When using the source code as a baseline, an incremental source generator is preferable because it reduces the performance overhead.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.ILocomotionContext<TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface enforcing the minimal information need for the context of locomotion state machine. It enforces the encapsulation configuration, input, and state data.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.ILocomotionState<TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface for defining locomotion states within a character movement state machine.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.ILocomotionStateMachine<TStateMachine, TStates, TContext, TProfile, TInput, TStateData>
files.Summary: Interface that defines the structure of a character locomotion state machine, responsible for managing state transitions, physics integration, collision resolution, and other locomotion behaviors of characters.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.LocomotionInput<TInput>
files.Summary: Stores a reference to the original PlayerInputAsset used by a player.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.LocomotionInputInitialized<TInput>
files.Summary: Stores a reference to the PlayerInputAsset instance of a player.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.LocomotionProfileBlob<TProfile>
files.Summary: Locomotion profile in the form of a blob asset. This profile contains configuration and parameters necessary for defining locomotion behavior within a state machine.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.LocomotionStateAttribute
files.Summary: This attribute mark an enum field as a Locomotion state. This is picked up by a source generate to generate the corresponding state machine system.

files.Title: Wayn.Locomotion.StateMachine.LocomotionStateMachineAttribute
files.Summary: This attribute mark an enum field as a Locomotion state. This is picked up by a source generate to generate the corresponding state machine system.
